content: Plan-quality rubric (issue #63 P8, slice 1) shipped as pure, INERT-on-the-loop functions in a NEW module plan-rubric.ts. P8 'spec & plan authoring' is burned down RUBRIC-FIRST — the same 'measure before optimizing' discipline as P7 (#62): you cannot prove a `plan` stage emits world-class plans, nor track the issue's success metric 'plan-completeness rubric score up across fixtures', without first a deterministic way to SCORE a plan. The rubric is a sibling to eval.ts's scoreTrajectory (a pure scorer over recorded data) but scores a plan/spec markdown DOCUMENT (text), not a run trajectory. scorePlanQuality(doc) checks the document against 8 criteria defined in the exported PLAN_CRITERIA array (fixed scorecard order): problem (a '#... Problem' heading), decisions (assumptions/decisions/rationale keyword), scopeGuard (scope guard/non-goals/out of scope/won't do), fileMap (a 'File map'/'File structure'/'Files'/'component map' HEADING OR >=2 path-like backticked tokens where path-like = contains '/' or a known source extension .ts/.js/.md/.json/.py/etc — so `pnpm -r test` is NOT miscounted as a path), taskBreakdown (>=2 markdown checkboxes OR >=2 top-of-line ordered items OR >=2 'Task' headings), testFirst (failing test/test-first/TDD/red-green/'write a test'/'pinned by'), verifyCommands (a 'verify:' label OR a verify mention AND a command token pnpm/npm run/vitest/tsc/node --test/pytest/go test/cargo test/make), successCriteria (success criteria|metric/acceptance criteria/done when/testing notes/testable). The issue's explicit four (scope guard? per-task verification? file map? testable success criteria?) are included PLUS the proven-shape essentials (problem, decisions, task breakdown, test-first) — scoring only the four would pass a plan with no problem statement or no tasks. Each detector is a PURE deterministic predicate (header/keyword regex heuristics, no tokenizer, no model call): the rubric judges STRUCTURAL completeness (does the plan have the sections a good plan has), the ORTHOGONAL question to the SEMANTIC quality a human/model judges at the checkpoint — heuristic and labelled as such, mirroring P7's ceil(chars/4) honesty. Returns PlanRubricScore { results: PlanCriterionResult[] (criterion,label,met), metCount, maxScore (=8), ratio (metCount/maxScore in 0..1, 0 when no criteria — no divide-by-zero), missing: string[] (labels of unmet criteria — the 'what to improve' surface a later slice feeds back into re-planning) }. Equal weights (YAGNI on a weighting scheme — the per-criterion breakdown lets a consumer reweight later without changing the scorer). formatPlanRubric(score) renders a 'plan quality: N/8 (P%)' header + a '[x]'/'[ ]' line per criterion label + a 'missing: ...' note when incomplete. Exported from index.ts (PLAN_CRITERIA, formatPlanRubric, scorePlanQuality + types PlanCriterion/PlanCriterionResult/PlanRubricScore). Wiring (capture as an eval signal alongside scoreTrajectory, an otto-afk --plan-report read-only surface, the dedicated `plan` stage + plan.md template emitting spec+plan under .otto/tasks/<key>/, the optional human checkpoint, a plan-quality gate that re-plans below a threshold) are later slices — substrate only, cannot regress a run. Pinned by plan-rubric.test.ts (7 tests). Substrate map confirmed before starting: no `plan` stage or rubric existed; STAGES has 8 stages (implementer/ghafk*/linear*/verifier/apply-review/reviewer), the brainstorm->spec->plan philosophy lives only as prose in the superpowers.md template fragment. Plan: .otto/tasks/issue-63/{spec,plan}.md (7 slices; this run = slice 1).
